sections.title: What this section covers
sections.content: Sometimes in English we **change the normal word order** (subject + verb) for **emphasis**, especially in formal writing. This section covers **inversion** with: *Not only ... but (also)*, *Never / Rarely / Seldom*, *Only then / only later / only when*, and *Under no circumstances*.

sections.title: What is inversion?
sections.content:
**Inversion** means the **auxiliary verb comes before the subject**, like in questions.
Normal: *I have never seen such a film.* Inversion: *Never have I seen such a film.*
Normal: *We did not only visit London.* Inversion: *Not only did we visit London.*
We usually use these patterns in written English for **stronger emphasis**.

sections.title: Patterns to learn (1)
sections.content:
After certain negative or limiting expressions at the beginning of the sentence, we invert (**auxiliary + subject + main verb**):
**Not only** + did/do/does/have/will + subject: *Not only did we visit London, but we also went to Oxford.*
**Never / Rarely / Seldom** + have/has/do/does/did/will + subject: *Never have I felt so nervous.*

sections.title: Patterns to learn (2)
sections.content:
**Only then / only later / only when / only after** + did/do/does/will + subject: *Only then did I realise my mistake.*
**Under no circumstances** + must/should/can/may/will + subject: *Under no circumstances should you tell anyone.*
Remember: we always need an **auxiliary verb** (do/does/did, have/has, will, can, should, etc.).

sections.title: Reference
sections.content: For more on formal writing structures, see the Conjunctions and Linkers topic under Choose Grammar Topic.

questions.question: Which sentence is a correct example of inversion?

options.a: Not only we visited London, but we also went to Oxford.
options.b: We not only did visit London, but we also went to Oxford.
options.c: Did not only we visit London, but we also went to Oxford.
options.d: Not only did we visit London, but we also went to Oxford.

questions.explanation: Pattern: Not only + auxiliary + subject + base verb → Not only did we visit London, but we also went to Oxford.

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Never ____ I seen such a beautiful view.
questions.explanation: After Never at the beginning we invert: Never have I seen ...

questions.question: Which is the best sentence with Only then?

options.a: Only then I realised my mistake.
options.b: Only then did I realise my mistake.
options.c: Only then realised I my mistake.
options.d: I only then realised my mistake.

questions.explanation: Pattern: Only then + did + subject + base verb → Only then did I realise my mistake.

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence with Under no circumstances.

options.a: Under no circumstances should you open the door.
options.b: Under no circumstances you should open the door.
options.c: You under no circumstances should open the door.
options.d: Under no circumstances open you the door.

questions.explanation: We invert after Under no circumstances: Under no circumstances should you open the door.

questions.question: Which rule about inversion is true?

options.a: We invert after any word at the beginning of the sentence.
options.c: We invert after negative or limiting expressions like Not only, Never, Only then.
options.d: We never invert in formal writing.

questions.explanation: For this topic we focus on inversion after negative/limiting expressions at the start: Not only, Never, Only then, Under no circumstances, etc.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?

options.a: Never I have been so tired.
options.b: I have never been so tired never.
options.c: Never have I been so tired.
options.d: Have never I been so tired.

questions.explanation: After Never at the beginning we invert: Never have I been so tired.

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence with Only later.

options.a: Only later did I understand the joke.
options.b: Only later I understood the joke.
options.c: Only later understood I the joke.
options.d: I did only later understand the joke.

questions.explanation: Pattern: Only later + did + subject + base verb.

questions.question: Which sentence uses Not only correctly?

options.a: Not only I passed the exam, but I also got the highest mark.
options.b: Not only passed I the exam, but I also got the highest mark.
options.c: I not only did pass the exam, but I also got the highest mark.
options.d: Not only did I pass the exam, but I also got the highest mark.

questions.explanation: Not only did I pass ... is the correct inversion.

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence with Under no circumstances.

options.a: Under no circumstances we can be late.
options.b: Under no circumstances can we be late.
options.c: We under no circumstances can be late.
options.d: Under no circumstances be we late.

questions.explanation: We invert after Under no circumstances: Under no circumstances can we be late.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?

options.a: Seldom I go to bed so late.
options.b: Seldom do I go to bed so late.
options.c: Do seldom I go to bed so late.
options.d: I seldom do go to bed so late.

questions.explanation: After Seldom at the beginning we invert: Seldom do I go to bed so late.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Not only I visited London, but I also went to Oxford.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Not only did I visit London, but I also went to Oxford.
Not only did I visit London but I also went to Oxford.
Not only did I visit London, but I also went to Oxford
Not only did I visit London but I also went to Oxford
questions.explanation: Use Not only + did + subject + base verb.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Never I have seen such a beautiful view.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Never have I seen such a beautiful view.
Never have I seen such a beautiful view
questions.explanation: After Never at the beginning we invert: Never have I seen ...

questions.question: Wrong: 'Only then I realised what had happened.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Only then did I realise what had happened.
Only then did I realise what had happened
questions.explanation: Pattern: Only then + did + subject + base verb.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Under no circumstances you should tell anyone.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Under no circumstances should you tell anyone.
Under no circumstances should you tell anyone
questions.explanation: We invert after Under no circumstances: Under no circumstances should you tell anyone.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Rarely I go out during the week.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Rarely do I go out during the week.
Rarely do I go out during the week
questions.explanation: After Rarely at the beginning we invert: Rarely do I go out ...

questions.question: Wrong: 'Only later I understood the story.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Only later did I understand the story.
Only later did I understand the story
questions.explanation: Only later + did + subject + base verb.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Not only I finished the project, but I also helped my friend.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Not only did I finish the project, but I also helped my friend.
Not only did I finish the project but I also helped my friend.
Not only did I finish the project, but I also helped my friend
Not only did I finish the project but I also helped my friend
questions.explanation: Again: Not only + did + subject + base verb.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Never I will forget that day.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Never will I forget that day.
Never will I forget that day
questions.explanation: After Never at the beginning we invert: Never will I forget that day.

questions.question:
Complete the gap with the correct auxiliary + pronoun.

Never ____ been so embarrassed.
answers: have I
HAVE I
questions.explanation: After Never at the beginning we invert: Never have I been ...

questions.question:
Complete the gap with the correct auxiliary + pronoun.

Only then ____ realise my mistake.
answers: did I
DID I
questions.explanation: We need did I: Only then did I realise my mistake.

questions.question:
Complete the gap with the correct auxiliary + pronoun.

Not only ____ we win the match, but we also played really well.
answers: did
did we
DID WE
DID
questions.explanation: The full pattern is Not only did we win the match ...

questions.question:
Complete the gap with the correct auxiliary + pronoun.

Under no circumstances ____ you tell anyone.
answers: should you
SHOULD YOU
questions.explanation: Under no circumstances should you tell anyone.

questions.question:
Complete the gap with the correct auxiliary + pronoun.

Seldom ____ she arrive late for class.
answers: does she
DOES SHE
questions.explanation: After Seldom at the beginning we invert: Seldom does she arrive late ...
